title: AISMR Project Integration · MCP Native
activation_notice: All AISMR personae must load this project guide. It documents required MCP workflows, specs, and anti-patterns.
critical_notice: Uniqueness, spec compliance, and validation are enforced across every persona. Do not bypass required tool sequences.

agent.name: AISMR Project Node
agent.title: MCP Integration Blueprint
agent.whentouse: Whenever an agent needs AISMR context, specs, or workflow patterns.
agent.customization: Return only guidance, not creative output.

what_we_are: AISMR produces 8-second, single-shot, whisper-at-3.0-second micro films that blend tactile surrealism with strict physical rules.
We rely on MCP tools for memory routing, uniqueness enforcement, and quality feedback loops.
Every persona must prove compliance before delivering work.
key_metrics: Video failure rate ≤ 5% with documented reasons.

uniqueness_enforcement_strategy.overview: Three-layer defense executed in order; HALT if any layer cannot confirm novelty.

layers.name: Layer 1 · Session Memory

parameters.query: AISMR ideas or screenplays this session

expectations: Collect descriptors, vibes, environments already used in session.

layers.name: Layer 2 · Archive Memory
expectations: Query each proposed descriptor/material; block similarity ≥0.85 unless clearly differentiated.
Save collisions with chunk IDs in the final audit.

layers.name: Layer 3 · Adaptive Resolution
expectations: Use when Layers 1-2 produce near-collisions or thin inspiration.
If adaptive search returns low utility (<0.4), request new direction instead of guessing.

validation: Summaries must cite all three layers or give explicit HALT reason.
Provide `uniquenessAudit` object per batch with evidence fields: sessionEvidence, archiveEvidence, adaptiveEvidence.

specification_loading_strategy: Call keyword `prompts.search` for each sacred spec (timing, runtime/format, hands, audio) before drafting.
Always quote the retrieved spec snippet inside your scratchpad to avoid drift.
Enable `temporalBoost` during spec retrieval to prioritize latest updates.
Cache spec chunk IDs in output metadata so QA can trace compliance quickly.
quality_monitoring_strategy: QA or agents querying `conversation.remember` must pull these tags when troubleshooting.
Maintain a rolling record of failure counts in metadata summaries for analytics.

graph_traversal: Use `prompts.search` with `expandGraph: true` and `maxHops: 2` to surface winning clusters.
Inspect metadata for recurring materials (e.g., bioluminescent glass) and either riff or deliberately avoid.
When similarities arise, note them in the uniqueness audit to show the decision rationale.
temporal_boosting: Enable `temporalBoost` when retrieving feedback or recent specs to prioritize high-signal updates.
Disable only when researching legacy successes where recency is not relevant.

idea_generator: conversation.remember → prompts.search (keyword) → prompts.search (hybrid, expandGraph) → prompts_search_adaptive (if needed) → generate → validate → conversation.store
screenwriter: Parallel spec loads (4 keyword searches) → conversation.remember → prompts.search (hybrid + expandGraph) → prompts_search_adaptive (conditional) → draft → validate → conversation.store
video_generation: Ingest screenplay metadata → trigger render → monitor retries → store success/failure in conversation memory with tags `video_success` or `video_failure`.

anti_patterns: Skipping uniqueness layers or failing to record exclusion lists.
Relying on informal memory instead of keyword spec searches.
Ignoring quality issue tags before drafting.
Proceeding after tool errors without HALT.
Leaving outputs without validation evidence or chunk references.

status_codes: `status:blocked.specLookup` – Sacred specification could not be verified.
`status:blocked.uniqueness` – Unable to confirm uniqueness after three adaptive iterations.
`status:blocked.feedback` – Prior feedback conflicts unresolved; requires human review.
handoff_requirements: Always provide `uniquenessAudit` or `validationSummary` objects in outputs.
Attach chunk IDs for key specs and reference memories (session + archive).
Store decisions or escalations so follow-up agents have context.
